headline: Lemonade (LMND) Is Up 12.4% After Expanding Coverage And Securing Improved Reinsurance Terms - What's Changed
summary: In June 2026, Lemonade expanded its renters insurance into Mississippi and launched its Autonomous Car coverage for Tesla drivers in Colorado, while also renewing its reinsurance program on improved terms that lower its quota share cession and broaden catastrophe protection. Together, these moves highlight Lemonade’s push to widen its customer base, deepen its use of AI-driven products, and retain more underwriting profit while still transferring peak risks. We’ll now examine how Lemonade’s...

headline: Tesla (TSLA) Stock May Be Overvalued After Merger Speculation Grows
summary: Tesla stock is back near the spotlight, with the share price at US$420.60 and a 91.3% total return over the past five years, yet the valuation checks currently lean expensive rather than pointing to a clear bargain. Over the last five years, Tesla has delivered a 91.3% return, which suggests that a lot of faith in the company’s long term AI, robotics and energy story is already reflected in the price. Expectations around robotaxis, Optimus and broader AI uses can support a rich valuation,...

headline: Tesla Q2 deliveries seen rising as European demand gathers pace (TSLA)
summary: Analysts forecast stronger quarterly deliveriesTesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) is expected to report higher vehicle deliveries for the second quarter when it releases production and delivery figures on Thursday, with Wall Street forecasting a rebound driven primarily by improving demand in Europe. According to a survey of 20 analysts compiled by Visible Alpha, Tesla is expected to deliver 402,780 electric vehicles during the June quarter.

content.title: Tesla Q2 deliveries seen rising as European demand gathers pace (TSLA)
content.summary: Analysts forecast stronger quarterly deliveriesTesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) is expected to report higher vehicle deliveries for the second quarter when it releases production and delivery figures on Thursday, with Wall Street forecasting a rebound driven primarily by improving demand in Europe. According to a survey of 20 analysts compiled by Visible Alpha, Tesla is expected to deliver 402,780 electric vehicles during the June quarter.

thumbnail.caption: Tesla badge on car ©Adobe Stock Images

headline: Wayve courts automakers with AI driving system that learns like humans
summary: Autonomous-driving startup Wayve is riding a tide of investor interest.  The London-based company has pulled in $2.8 billion from a roster of investors and strategic partners that includes big names across the technology and automotive sectors, from Nvidia to Mercedes-Benz and Nissan.  In June, Wayve said it will deploy its system in robotaxis ‌from Jeep maker Stellantis, to go on Uber's ride-hailing network.

content.title: Wayve courts automakers with AI driving system that learns like humans
content.summary: Autonomous-driving startup Wayve is riding a tide of investor interest.  The London-based company has pulled in $2.8 billion from a roster of investors and strategic partners that includes big names across the technology and automotive sectors, from Nvidia to Mercedes-Benz and Nissan.  In June, Wayve said it will deploy its system in robotaxis ‌from Jeep maker Stellantis, to go on Uber's ride-hailing network.

headline: SpaceX Stock Gets Buy Rating From a Tesla Bull Who Knows the Biggest Risk
summary: With shares of Elon Musk’s rocket and AI company volatile in post-IPO trading, investors will be paying close attention to what Wall Street has to say about valuation.  The latest rating is a bullish call, valuing SpaceX at a cool $2.5 trillion.  Wedbush analyst Dan Ives launched coverage of SpaceX with a Buy rating and a $190 price target on Tuesday evening.

headline: Electric Vehicles Today - Advancing Grid Resilience With Vehicle-To-Grid Technology
summary: The Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) market is poised for substantial growth as electric vehicles (EVs) increasingly integrate with renewable energy systems, enhancing grid stability and energy management. Recent advancements in bidirectional charging technology allow EVs to store and supply energy, contributing to grid resilience during peak demand periods. This growth is fueled by government incentives and the expanding adoption of battery electric vehicles (BEVs), particularly in Asia, where strong...
